rows.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of unease that a hidden weakness in the validation will surface after sign-off
rows.imp_rat: Unease about hidden validation weaknesses matters but is a derived emotional state rather than the functional deliverable.
rows.sat_rat: Peer review and QA approval mitigate this somewhat, yet post-sign-off surprises remain a known fear.

rows.source_job: feel reassured the line keeps flowing
rows.stmt: Reduce the likelihood of anxiety that the added verification will stall the line on this stakeholder's watch
rows.imp_rat: Anxiety about the verification stalling the line on their watch reflects a real reputational pressure but is secondary to the technical failure modes.
rows.sat_rat: Proven reliable systems ease this worry, but any line the engineer just integrated still carries live stall anxiety until it proves stable.

rows.stmt: Reduce the amount of concession given away on price and warranty terms during negotiation for the check-weighing system
rows.imp_rat: Limiting concessions on price and warranty affects value captured but is secondary to securing a compliant, performing system for this specialized buy.
rows.sat_rat: Negotiation playbooks give moderate leverage, but limited vendor competition in niche check-weighing weakens the manager's concession position.

rows.stmt: Reduce the time taken to obtain capital authorization for the check-weighing acquisition from financial decision authorities
rows.imp_rat: Speed of capital authorization matters to keep the project on track, but timeliness is secondary to getting the funding and terms right.
rows.sat_rat: Approval cycles for capital in regulated pharma are structured but slow, giving moderate but not strong satisfaction on speed.

rows.stmt: Diminish the degree to which leadership doubts the steward secured the strongest commercial terms on the check-weighing deal
rows.imp_rat: Leadership doubt about whether the best terms were secured affects the manager's standing as a trusted capital steward.
rows.sat_rat: Benchmarking and negotiation documentation partly demonstrate diligence, but limited market comparators leave lingering doubt.

rows.stmt: Reduce the frequency of transcription errors requiring correction in documented weight-verification results
rows.imp_rat: Transcription errors in verification records create rework and data-integrity findings the technician must correct.
rows.sat_rat: Automated electronic capture has cut transcription errors substantially versus manual logging today.

rows.stmt: Minimize the frequency of dispute resolutions that are later overturned by additional review
rows.imp_rat: Overturned dispute resolutions waste review cycles and signal weak conformance criteria, but they are a less frequent part of the QA Manager's day than release assurance.
rows.sat_rat: Established deviation and CAPA workflows usually settle disputes durably, yet borderline weight cases can still be reopened on further review.

rows.stmt: Minimize the number of approval rejections caused by incomplete authorization documentation
rows.imp_rat: Rejections from incomplete authorization documentation delay funding and erode the director's credibility, though they are procedural rather than value-destroying.
rows.sat_rat: Established capital-request templates and stage-gate checklists in mature pharma finance functions handle documentation completeness reasonably well.

rows.stmt: Minimize the percentage of in-specification containers rejected during weight verification
rows.imp_rat: Rejecting good containers wastes costly sterile product, which the supervisor is judged on, though it is a lesser sin than passing bad ones.
rows.sat_rat: Current systems set conservative reject limits that over-reject to stay safe, so false rejects of in-spec units are a routine tolerated loss.

rows.stmt: Reduce the degree of measurement scatter that pushes acceptable containers across the rejection threshold
rows.imp_rat: Measurement scatter that wrongly rejects acceptable units erodes yield the supervisor must protect, a moderate concern versus outright compliance risk.
rows.sat_rat: Vibration isolation and averaging on current check-weighers limit scatter, but in-motion weighing at line speed still introduces borderline misclassifications.

rows.stmt: Minimize the amount of operator attention diverted to sustaining weight verification across a production run
rows.imp_rat: The supervisor needs operators focused on aseptic technique, so attention consumed by babysitting weight verification is a real distraction, but it is secondary to actually catching OOS fills.
rows.sat_rat: Automated inline check-weighers already offload much routine attention, though periodic manual sampling and alarm handling still pull operators in on most current lines.

rows.stmt: Reduce the degree to which peers question the rigor behind the weight-verification calls attributed to this role
rows.imp_rat: Peers questioning the rigor of weight-verification calls touches professional reputation but is less critical than regulatory defensibility.
rows.sat_rat: Standardized procedures lend credibility, but subjective borderline calls can still invite peer scrutiny.

rows.stmt: Minimize the degree to which quality leadership questions the soundness of the role's validation judgment
rows.imp_rat: Leadership confidence in the engineer's judgment affects standing but is a status concern secondary to the technical job.
rows.sat_rat: Experienced engineers generally retain leadership trust, so this is moderately satisfied today.

rows.stmt: Minimize the frequency with which quality leadership must second-guess the recommended verification regime, diminishing perceived expertise
rows.imp_rat: Being second-guessed by quality leadership erodes perceived expertise, but it is a status concern secondary to the technical defensibility job.
rows.sat_rat: Established metrology credentials and documented regimes generally command respect, limiting routine second-guessing.

rows.stmt: Reduce the amount of oversight effort spent on verification points carrying negligible conformance risk
rows.imp_rat: Trimming oversight effort on negligible-risk verification points helps efficiency but is secondary to the QA Manager's core conformance-assurance stakes.
rows.sat_rat: Current review workflows are often blanket and procedure-driven, giving little risk-based prioritization to relieve low-value oversight.

rows.stmt: Minimize the percentage of calibration events performed more often than the measurement's demonstrated stability requires
rows.imp_rat: Over-calibration is a cost and resource concern the specialist weighs, but it does not threaten metrological defensibility the way under-calibration does.
rows.sat_rat: Conservative fixed intervals dominate the status quo, so many events run more frequently than stability data would justify.

rows.stmt: Reduce the time spent documenting weight-verification results during the production run
rows.imp_rat: Time spent documenting during the run is overhead that pulls the technician away from monitoring weights.
rows.sat_rat: Automated data logging has already reduced in-run documentation burden considerably today.

rows.stmt: Reduce the frequency of manual re-checks an operator must perform to confirm a container's weight during a run
rows.imp_rat: Frequent manual re-checks add operator burden and aseptic-intervention risk, but they are a workflow nuisance rather than a compliance core for the supervisor.
rows.sat_rat: Automated verification reduces manual re-checks, yet confirmatory re-weighs after alarms still recur on today's lines.
